===Pokémon=== [[File:Cynthia Party DP098.png|thumb|220px|Cynthia and her Pokémon joining forces in ''[[DP097|Double Team Turnover!]]'']] [[File:Cynthia {{#switch: {{#expr: {{#time: U}} mod 2}}|0=Key Stone|1=Dynamax Band}}.png|left|thumb|250px|Cynthia's {{#switch: {{#expr: {{#time: U}} mod 2}}|0=Key Stone|1=Dynamax Band}}]] The only permanent member of Cynthia's team is {{TP|Cynthia|Garchomp}}. Despite Cynthia not having a specialty type, she is well known for her affinity for {{type|Dragon}} Pokémon; because of this, Cynthia uses powerful Pokémon of that type like {{p|Garchomp}} and {{p|Kommo-o}}, which led to her being challenged by {{an|Iris}} in ''[[BW085|All for the Love of Meloetta!]]''. She also seems to work exceptionally well towards {{type|Ground}}; as shown in ''[[DP097|Double Team Turnover!]]'', when she combined the powers of both her Garchomp and {{p|Gastrodon}} against Team Galactic in addition to {{type|Water}} Pokémon in ''[[JN117|The Fiery Road to Mastership]]''; when she both used her Gastrodon and {{p|Milotic}} against Iris in their World Coronation Series Masters Eight Match. Cynthia is rarely seen using any other Pokémon besides her {{TP|Cynthia|Garchomp}}; however, in dire situations, she has resorted to other Pokémon on her team, and they appear similarly well-trained. For example, {{p|Gastrodon}} was able to easily defeat [[Aaron's Beautifly]] despite it being on the receiving end of a doubly [[Type#Type effectiveness|supereffective]] {{m|Solar Beam}}, {{p|Roserade}} singlehandedly defeated [[Team Galactic]]'s Pokémon, {{p|Spiritomb}} managed to defeat half of {{Ash}}'s team in battle, {{p|Togekiss}} was able to withstand several powerful supereffective {{m|Bullet Punch}}es from [[Ash's Lucario]], her {{p|Milotic}} easily defeated [[Iris's Dragonite]], and her Kommo-o thwarted [[Alternate World Team Rocket]]'s initial plans with a single {{m|Outrage}}. During her battle with Ash, Cynthia was revealed to have a [[Dynamax Band]] in addition to having a [[Key Stone]]. Cynthia used this primarily to cause unpredictability in battle and pushed several of Ash's Pokémon to the limit. This listing is of Cynthia's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: ====On hand==== {| width="100%" | {{ActivePoké|Cynthia|Garchomp|Cynthia Garchomp.png|dragon|ground|size=250px|disp=Egg → Gible → Gabite → Garchomp|link=Cynthia's Garchomp}} {{MegaIcon}} ♀ | | |} ====In rotation==== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Cynthia |pkmn=Gastrodon |type1=Water |type2=Ground |img=Cynthia Gastrodon.png |epnum=DP097 |epname=Double Team Turnover! |vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima |vaen=Tom Wayland |vaennote=[[DP097]]-[[DP100]] |vaen2=Marc Thompson |vaen2note=[[JN117]]-present |desc={{p|Gastrodon}} is Cynthia's second known Pokémon. It was first seen when it helped Cynthia in defeating [[Team Galactic]]'s {{p|Golbat}} to help protect the [[Lustrous Orb]]. It appeared again in ''[[DP100|Aiding the Enemy!]]'', where it helped Cynthia to defeat [[Aaron]]'s {{p|Drapion}} and {{TP|Aaron|Beautifly}} and defend her Championship title. In ''[[JN116|Pride of a Champion!]]'', Gastrodon is the first Pokémon Cynthia chose to use in her battle against Iris at the [[Masters Eight Tournament]]. In the [[JN117|following episode]], it fell to [[Iris's Excadrill]] after a hard-fought battle. In ''[[JN123|Bewitch, Battle, and Bewilder!]]'', Gastrodon was the fourth Pokémon used by Cynthia in her Masters Eight Tournament battle against {{Ash}}. It went up against {{AP|Pikachu}}, only to be defeated, despite the type advantage. Gastrodon's known moves are {{m|Stone Edge}}, {{m|Muddy Water}}, {{m|Rain Dance}}, {{m|Acid Armor}}, and {{m|Earth Power}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Cynthia |pkmn=Glaceon |type1=Ice |img=Cynthia Glaceon.png |epnum=BW085 |epname=All for the Love of Meloetta! |vajp=Inuko Inuyama |vaen=Inuko Inuyama |desc={{p|Glaceon}} is Cynthia's third known Pokémon. It was sent out to make ice for a sick {{an|Meloetta}}. Glaceon's only known move is {{m|Ice Beam}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Cynthia |pkmn=Kommo-o |type1=Dragon |type2=Fighting |img=Cynthia Kommo-o.png |epnum=JN089 |epname=The Gates of Warp! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Kommo-o}} is Cynthia's fourth known Pokémon. It was used to battle [[Alternate World Team Rocket]], who were trying to steal [[Ash's Pikachu]], breaking the electric nets they had fired. Kommo-o's only known move is {{m|Outrage}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Cynthia |pkmn=Roserade |type1=Grass |type2=Poison |img=Cynthia Roserade.png |epnum=JNS03 |epname=The Arceus Chronicles (Part 3) |vajp=Kei Shindō |vaen=Sarah Natochenny |desc={{p|Roserade}} is Cynthia's fifth known Pokémon. It was first used to battle the [[Team Galactic]] {{tc|Commander}}s alongside [[Brock's Croagunk]] and {{TP|Brock|Blissey}}. After defeating them, Cynthia had Roserade tie them up with its vines. Roserade made its main series debut in ''[[JN123|Bewitch, Battle, and Bewilder!]]'', where it was the second Pokémon Cynthia used in her [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}. It battled against {{AP|Gengar}} and got {{status|burn}}ed before being recalled. In [[JN124|the next episode]], it was chosen to battle against {{AP|Dracovish}}, showing that it got rid of its burn due to its [[Ability]] upon being recalled. Despite managing to {{status|poison}} Dracovish, Roserade was eventually defeated. Roserade's known moves are {{m|Petal Blizzard}}, {{m|Power Whip}}, {{m|Bullet Seed}}, {{m|Leaf Storm}}, and {{m|Poison Jab}}, and its [[Ability]] is {{a|Natural Cure}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Cynthia |pkmn=Milotic |stage1=Feebas |type1=Water |img=Cynthia Feebas.png |cap=Cynthia's Feebas |img2=Cynthia Milotic.png |cap2=Cynthia's Milotic |epnum=JN117 |epname=The Fiery Road to Mastership! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Milotic}} is Cynthia's sixth known Pokémon. It was the second Pokémon Cynthia used in her [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{an|Iris}}. It battled [[Iris's Excadrill]] and {{TP|Iris|Dragonite}}, defeating them before being defeated by her {{TP|Iris|Haxorus}}. In ''[[JN124|Valor: A Strategic Part of Battling!]]'', Cynthia used Milotic as her sixth Pokémon in her Masters Eight Tournament match against {{Ash}}. It finished off [[Ash's Dracovish]], but not without receiving massive damage from its {{m|Fishious Rend}} in the process. It subsequently battled {{AP|Sirfetch'd}}, but was defeated by a {{m|Meteor Assault}}. In ''[[JN125|Whittle While You Work!]]'', Milotic appeared as a {{p|Feebas}} in a flashback to Cynthia's childhood. Milotic's known moves are {{m|Hydro Pump}}, {{m|Disarming Voice}}, and {{m|Iron Head}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Cynthia |pkmn=Spiritomb |type1=Ghost |type2=Dark |img=Cynthia Spiritomb.png |epnum=JN123 |epname=Bewitch, Battle, and Bewilder! |vajp=Megumi Hayashibara |vaen=Ray Chase |desc={{p|Spiritomb}} is Cynthia's seventh known Pokémon. It was the first Pokémon Cynthia used in her [[Masters Eight Tournament]] semifinals match against {{Ash}}. It first went up against {{AP|Dragonite}} and managed to easily defeat it with a {{m|Hypnosis}}-{{m|Dream Eater}} combo, and later also defeated {{AP|Gengar}}. Once {{AP|Pikachu}} was up, however, its combo was neutered by a [[Counter Shield]] before finally being knocked out, but not without also taking Pikachu with it due to {{m|Destiny Bond}}. Spiritomb's known moves are {{m|Sucker Punch}}, {{m|Hypnosis}}, {{m|Dream Eater}}, and {{m|Destiny Bond}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Cynthia |pkmn=Togekiss [[File:Dynamax icon.png|30px|link=Dynamax]] |type1=Fairy |type2=Flying |img=Cynthia Togekiss.png |cap=Cynthia's Togekiss |img2=Cynthia Dynamax Togekiss.png |cap2=Cynthia's Dynamax Togekiss |epnum=JN123 |epname=Bewitch, Battle, and Bewilder! |vajp=Hana Takeda |vaen=Hana Takeda |desc={{p|Togekiss}} is Cynthia's eighth known Pokémon. It was the third Pokémon Cynthia used in her [[Masters Eight Tournament]] semifinals match against {{Ash}}. It briefly battled against his {{AP|Pikachu}} before being recalled. In [[JN124|the next episode]], Togekiss was brought out again to battle against {{AP|Lucario}}. After resisting several of Lucario's {{m|Bullet Punch}}es, Cynthia [[Dynamax]]ed Togekiss. In ''[[JN125|Whittle While You Work!]]'', Cynthia had Togekiss used {{m|Max Airstream}} repeatedly, which gave it a speed boost after its Dynamax turns were over that allowed it to overwhelm Ash's {{me|Lucario}} for a while, before being knocked out by a well-timed {{m|Bullet Punch}}. Togekiss's known moves are {{m|Air Slash}}, {{m|Zen Headbutt}}, and {{m|Sky Attack}}, and its [[Ability]] is {{a|Serene Grace}}.
As Dynamax Togekiss, it can use {{m|Max Airstream}}.}} ====Other==== {{TrainerPoké |nick=Oshawott, Rowlet, and Cyndaquil |type1=Hisui |img=Hisuian first partners anime.png |epnum=JNS01 |epname=The Arceus Chronicles (Part 1) |desc=Cynthia offered to {{Ash}}, {{an|Dawn}}, and [[Goh]] a choice between {{p|Oshawott}}, {{p|Rowlet}}, and {{p|Cyndaquil}}. These Pokémon took part in a catching competition, where the three Trainers must catch them within three minutes. Cynthia also revealed that these Pokémon are meant to help her learn more about the [[Hisui|Sinnoh region's past]] and [[Regional form|their effects]] on these Pokémon. Cyndaquil's only known move is {{m|Flamethrower}}.
None of Oshawott and Rowlet's moves are known.}} ====Temporary==== {{TrainerPoké |main=Brock's Croagunk |trainer=Brock |pkmn=Croagunk |gender=male |type1=Poison |type2=Fighting |img=Cynthia Blissey Croagunk.png |epnum=DP008 |epname=Gymbaliar! |vajp=Katsuyuki Konishi |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{an|Brock}} lent his {{p|Croagunk}} to Cynthia to battle the [[Team Galactic]] {{tc|Commander}}s while he went to help {{Ash}}, {{an|Dawn}}, and [[Goh]].}} {{TrainerPoké |main=Brock's Blissey |trainer=Brock |pkmn=Blissey |gender=female |type1=Normal |img=Cynthia Blissey Croagunk.png |epnum=DP038 |epname=One Big Happiny Family! |vajp= |vaen=Sarah Natochenny |desc={{an|Brock}} lent his {{p|Blissey}} to Cynthia to battle the [[Team Galactic]] {{tc|Commander}}s while he went to help {{Ash}}, {{an|Dawn}}, and [[Goh]].}}
